revision.text:
[[ja:愛宕山横穴墓群]][[zh:愛宕山橫穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site}} {{Japanese Kofuns| | name = Atagoyama Yokonahaka Kofun Group | location = Mukaigaoka 4-chome, Taihaku-ku, Sendai, Miyagi Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|38|14|43.5|N|140|52|39|E|display=inline|qq=}} | type = Horizontal cave tombs (33 confirmed, some decorated tombs) | built = Late 7th century – Early 8th century | area = | Buried Individuals = | designation1 = | burial goods = Iron swords, iron arrowheads, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}, glass beads, etc. | image =
Current condition of cave openings on the northern slope, two of many tombs | image = AtagoyamaYokoanaBogun2006-9a.jpg }} '''Atagoyama Yokonahaka Kofun Group''' is a collection of numerous [[Yokoanabo|horizontal cave tombs]] from the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} located in {{ill|Sendai|en|Sendai|qid=Q46747|lt=Sendai|1=Sendai}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|qid=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}, Japan, built into the slopes of {{ill|Mount Atago (Sendai)|ja|愛宕山 (仙台市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Atago (Sendai)|1=Mount Atago|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11493829}}. Some tombs remain open to this day, while others have been excavated from underground, and some were destroyed without being surveyed. The total number of tombs is unknown. One of these tombs was a decorated burial chamber painted with red {{ill|Pigment|en|Pigment|qid=Q161179|lt=pigments|1=Pigment}}. == Site Overview == [[image:AtagoyamaYokoanaBogun2006-9b.jpg|thumb|240px|none|Left-side cave]] [[image:AtagoyamaYokoanaBogun2006-9c.jpg|thumb|240px|none|Right-side cave (Photos taken in September 2006)]]{{ill|Mount Atago (Sendai)|ja|愛宕山 (仙台市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Atago (Sendai)|1=Mount Atago|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11493829}} is a low mountain extending into the {{ill|Sendai Plain|en|Sendai Plain|qid=Q10884198|lt=Sendai Plain|1=Sendai Plain}} from the western hills, located along the {{ill|Hirose River (Miyagi Prefecture)|ja|広瀬川 (宮城県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirose River (Miyagi Prefecture)|1=Hirose River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11485581}}. The Atagoyama Yokonahaka Kofun Group consists of numerous horizontal cave tombs on the northern and southeastern slopes of this mountain. On the southern side, across from {{ill|Ōkuboyachi|ja|大窪谷地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuboyachi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, there are also horizontal tombs on {{ill|Mount Daidenji|ja|大年寺山|1=Mount Daidenji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Daidenji|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Daidenji|qq=}}, which are known as the {{ill|Daidenjiyama Yokonahaka Kofun Group|ja|大年寺山横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daidenjiyama Yokonahaka Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135502382|1=Daidenjiyama Yokonahaka Kofun Group}}. Additionally, the {{ill|Futatsusawa Yokonahaka Kofun Group|ja|二ツ沢横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futatsusawa Yokonahaka Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135502384|1=Futatsusawa Yokonahaka Kofun Group}}, the {{ill|Mogasaki Yokonahaka Kofun Group|ja|茂ヶ崎横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogasaki Yokonahaka Kofun Group|1=Mogasaki Yokoanabo Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11617020}}, and the {{ill|Sōzenji Yokonahaka Kofun Group|ja|宗禅寺横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōzenji Yokonahaka Kofun Group|1=Sōzenji Yokoanabo Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11452025}} are found along the Hirose River, all collectively referred to as the {{ill|Mukaigaoka Yokonahaka Kofun Group|ja|向山横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukaigaoka Yokonahaka Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135502386|1=Mukaigaoka Yokonahaka Kofun Group}}. These tombs are believed to have been constructed from the late 7th century to the early 8th century. Approximately 2 kilometers southeast of this site is the {{ill|Sendai Kōriyama Kanga ruins|en|Sendai Kōriyama Kanga ruins|qid=Q11643209|lt=Kōriyama Ruins|1=Sendai Kōriyama Kanga ruins}}, where the provincial capital of {{ill|Mutsu Province|en|Mutsu Province|qid=Q907389|lt=Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province}} was located at the time. It is speculated that these tombs were built by the people who lived in the capital and surrounding settlements. {{right| }} In {{ill|1938|en|1938|qid=Q18645|lt=1938|1=1938}} (Shōwa 13), a total of 34 tombs were found on the northern side of {{ill|Mount Daidenji|ja|大年寺山|1=Mount Daidenji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Daidenji|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Daidenji|qq=}} and on both the north and south slopes of Mount Atago. Additionally, it is reported that over ten more tombs were destroyed within the next few years. A map from the time shows three tombs on the northern slope of Atagoyama, three on the eastern slope, 20 on the southeastern slope, and nine other unidentified or destroyed tombs. The exact locations of the tombs recorded in this survey are now unknown, as many were lost due to residential development and road construction, making it impossible to determine their precise number and placement.Shimizu Toshiro, "Ancient Monuments and Horizontal Tombs of Miyagi Prefecture" (宮城県の古碑及横穴). The accompanying map was hand-drawn and does not accurately reflect the modern altered landscape. The text states that 34 tombs exist, but only 33 are depicted with slanted lines, while other types of tombs or destroyed tombs are shown without explanation. Since {{ill|1973|en|1973|qid=Q2477|lt=1973|1=1973}} (Shōwa 48), surveys have focused on the tombs on the southern slope, where 28 tombs have been confirmed. In addition, a single horizontal tomb with red murals was found near the southeastern slope. No excavations have been conducted on the northern side. These tombs were built into the middle of the slopes, with a passage ({{ill|yūdo|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yūdo|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}}) approximately 1 meter wide leading to a slightly narrower entrance ({{ill|genmon|ja|玄門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/genmon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), which then opens into the burial chamber ({{ill|genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/genshitsu|qq=}}). Some tombs lack an entrance door. The length of the passage is unknown. The burial chambers are either rectangular or trapezoidal, sometimes with rounded corners, and most range from 1 to 3 meters per side. One tomb was found with stones laid over half of its floor. Due to poor preservation conditions, the full range of burial goods is unclear. However, burial goods such as iron swords, iron arrowheads, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}, and {{ill|glass beads|ja|ガラス玉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/glass beads|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to とんぼ玉|1=Glass bead making|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1435588}} have been found. == Atagoyama Decorated Yokonahaka Kofun == Near the open tombs, a decorated horizontal tomb was discovered underground. The entrance passage ({{ill|yūdo|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yūdo|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}}) was 2.5 meters long and 1.15 meters wide at the bottom. The burial chamber ({{ill|genshitsu|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/genshitsu|qq=}}) was nearly square, measuring 1.8 meters (front), 1.9 meters (back), 1.8 meters (right), and 1.65 meters (left). The chamber is estimated to be about 1.4 meters high and shaped like either a house or a dome. The entrance appears to have been sealed with stacked stones, possibly with a door that has since disappeared. The construction of this tomb follows the same pattern as the others in the group. The back wall of the burial chamber features decorations painted in red pigment, consisting of multiple circular and cross-shaped motifs arranged in a scattered pattern. Grave goods found in this tomb included one {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} vessel, one small knife, the remains of five individuals, as well as unidentified iron objects, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} fragments. This decorated tomb is estimated to date to the mid-to-late 7th century. The presence of murals suggests that a high-Ranking individual may have been buried here. == Excavation History == The open cave tombs of {{ill|Mount Atago (Sendai)|ja|愛宕山 (仙台市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Atago (Sendai)|1=Mount Atago|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11493829}} have been known locally for a long time. A map published in {{ill|1912|en|1912|qid=Q2077|lt=1912|1=1912}} (Taishō 1) records those on the northern side as {{ill|Ezo caves|lt=Ezo caves|lt=Emishi caves|ja|蝦夷穴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi caves|qq=}}.『最新版仙台全図』（『100th year 前の仙台を歩く　仙台地図散歩』60頁）。 In {{ill|1936|en|1936|qid=Q18649|lt=1936|1=1936}} (Shōwa 11), during road construction that cut into the mountain, numerous horizontal tombs were discovered, along with human remains, straight swords, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}. However, all of these finds were lost.Shimizu Toshiro, "Ancient Monuments and Horizontal Tombs of Miyagi Prefecture" (宮城県の古碑及横穴), p. 28. Ito Nobuo, "Ancient Ruins of Sendai City" (仙台市内の古代遺跡), p. 73. The first systematic investigation of Atagoyama’s cave tombs was conducted by {{ill|Shimizu Toshiro|ja|清水東四郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu Toshiro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|1937|en|1937|qid=Q18647|lt=1937|1=1937}} (Shōwa 12), when he reported a total of 34 tombs on the southeastern and northern slopes.Shimizu Toshiro, "Ancient Monuments and Horizontal Tombs of Miyagi Prefecture" (宮城県の古碑及横穴), pp. 27–28. Later, {{ill|Ito Nobuo|ja|伊東信雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ito Nobuo|1=Nobuo Itō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q25983037}} also took measurements of one of the tombs.Ito Nobuo, "Ancient Ruins of Sendai City" (仙台市内の古代遺跡), pp. 72–73. The exact date of this survey is not mentioned in the text and may have been postwar. During World War II, the open tombs were repurposed as air-raid shelters, and after the war, some were used as household storage spaces. As Sendai expanded, urban development destroyed additional tombs. When {{ill|Atago Bridge (Sendai)|ja|愛宕大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Bridge (Sendai)|1=Atago Great Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11493826}} was constructed, its connecting roads cut into the mountain's edge, prompting an official excavation by the Sendai City authorities. In {{ill|1973|en|1973|qid=Q2477|lt=1973|1=1973}}, ten cave tombs were confirmed on the southern slope, and nine of them were excavated. During this survey, a single {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} vessel and human bones were discovered.『仙台市向山愛宕山横穴群発掘調査報告書』。 On April 21, {{ill|1976|en|1976|qid=Q2480|lt=1976|1=1976}} (Shōwa 51), roadwork near the southeastern slope suddenly revealed a tomb containing human bones. The {{ill|Sendai South Police Station|ja|仙台南警察署|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai South Police Station|1=Sendai South Police Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11377794}} was notified and determined that the remains were ancient, prompting the Sendai City Board of Education to conduct an emergency excavation. This tomb turned out to be the decorated horizontal Kofun, the most significant discovery among the Atagoyama tombs. Although the chamber’s wall paintings were recognized as an important find, there were no resources for preservation at the time, and the tomb was reburied after documentation.『宮城県仙台市愛宕山装飾横穴古墳発掘調査報告書』。 The site is located just outside the curve of {{ill|Hirosegawa Riverside Road|ja|広瀬河畔通り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirosegawa Riverside Road|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広瀬河畔通|1=Hirosegawa Riverside Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11485616}}. In September and October of {{ill|1991|en|1991|qid=Q2062|lt=1991|1=1991}} (Heisei 3), an excavation was conducted before the construction of new housing. This revealed 18 underground cave tombs, of which 15 were investigated. Many of these had been partially damaged before the excavation, but burial goods such as straight swords and glass beads were still recovered.『仙台市愛宕山横穴墓群 第3次発掘調査報告書』。 == References == {{reflist|qq=}} == External links == * [https://web.archive.org/web/20130615090741/http://www.city.sendai.jp/Category/bunka/02.html Historic Site of Sendai] ({{ill|Sendai City Board of Education|ja|仙台市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai City Board of Education|qq=}}) ''' [http://www.city.sendai.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/iseki/c0000000005.html Atagoyama Yokonahaka Kofun Group] == References == {{reflist|qq=}} == Distribution and Notable Yokoanabo Tombs == * {{ill|Atagoyama Yokoanabos|ja|愛宕山横穴墓群|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atagoyama Yokoanabos|qq=}} ({{ill|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taihaku-ku, Sendai|qq=}}, {{ill|Sendai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai|qq=}}) * {{ill|Mogasaki Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogasaki Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) - 25 tombs, no longer extant * {{ill|Sōzenji Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōzenji Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) * {{ill|Kiyotosaku Yokoanabo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyotosaku Yokoanabo|qq=}} ({{ill|Futaba, Fukushima|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futaba, Fukushima|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|qid=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Izumizaki Yokoanabo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumizaki Yokoanabo|qq=}} ({{ill|Nishishirakawa District|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishishirakawa District|qq=}}, {{ill|Izumizaki, Fukushima|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumizaki, Fukushima|qq=}}) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nakata Yokoanabos|en|Nakata Yokoanabos|qid=Q18337575|lt=Nakata Yokoanabos|1=Nakata Yokoanabos}} ({{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaki, Fukushima|qq=}}) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Hayama Yokoanabo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Yokoanabo|qq=}} ({{ill|Minamisōma|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamisōma|qq=}}, Fukushima Prefecture) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Wada Daibutsu and Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada Daibutsu and Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Sukagawa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa|qq=}}, Fukushima Prefecture) - Municipal Historic Site === Kantō Region === * {{ill|Jūgorō-ana Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgorō-ana Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Hitachinaka, Ibaraki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachinaka, Ibaraki|qq=}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|qid=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}) - Over 300 tombs * {{ill|Karanogosho Yokoanabo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karanogosho Yokoanabo|qq=}} ({{ill|Nasukaragawa, Tochigi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasukaragawa, Tochigi|qq=}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|qid=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site, part of the Hokōda and Wami Yokoanabo Clusters * {{ill|Nagaoka Hundred Caves|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}} ({{ill|Utsunomiya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture) - 52 tombs * {{ill|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshimi Hundred Caves|qq=}} ({{ill|Yoshimi, Saitama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshimi, Saitama|qq=}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|qid=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Kuroiwa Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroiwa Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} (Yoshimi, Saitama) - Over 30 tombs * {{ill|Jūrō Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūrō Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Hatoyama, Saitama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatoyama, Saitama|qq=}}, Saitama) - 3 tombs * {{ill|Takinojō Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takinojō Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Tokorozawa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokorozawa|qq=}}, Saitama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitaakitsu Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bikuniyama Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashimatsuyama|qq=}}, Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takane Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Kumagaya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagaya|qq=}}, Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagara Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagara, Chiba|qq=}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Graves|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deyama Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitaka, Tokyo|qq=}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakawada Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama, Tokyo|qq=}}, Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Todoroki Ravine Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Setagaya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya|qq=}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekoda Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoba-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, {{ill|Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichigao Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}} (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsugasaki Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Yokoanabo Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichisekiyama Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoshigaya Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs === Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Yokoanabos|en|Kashiya Yokoanabos|qid=Q22118267|lt=Kashiya Yokoanabos|1=Kashiya Yokoanabos}} ({{ill|Kannami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannami|qq=}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|qid=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitaema Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Izunokuni|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunokuni|qq=}}, Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/E-no-ura Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Numazu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numazu|qq=}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs === Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Yokoanabos|en|Hōōzan Yokoanabos|qid=Q18339249|lt=Hōōzan Yokoanabos|1=Hōōzan Cave Tombs}} ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga, Ishikawa|qq=}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|qid=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs === Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Yokoanabos|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaida Yokoanabos|qq=}} ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwara, Osaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfukuji Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs === Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūōmen Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Matsue|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsue|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|qid=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture|1=Shimane Prefecture}}) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsunedani Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikonishi Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo, Shimane|qq=}}, Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuku Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chibu, Shimane|qq=}}, [[:en:Oki District|Oki District]]) === Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenami Site|qq=}} ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukuhashi|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiroyama Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuchi, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizumachi Site|qq=}} ({{ill|Nōgata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nōgata|qq=}}, Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoanabo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kozuki Yokoanabo|qq=}} ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurate, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishinuki Nagino Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Tamana|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamana|qq=}}, {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|qid=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabeta Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga, Kumamoto|qq=}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmura Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitoyoshi|qq=}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Yokoanabo Cluster|en|Yokkaichi Yokoanabo Cluster|qid=Q11419962|lt=Yokkaichi Yokoanabo Cluster|1=Yokkaichi Yokoanabo Cluster}} ({{ill|Usa (宇佐)|lt=Usa (宇佐)|lt=Usa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|qid=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoanabo Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takio Hyakka Yokoanabo Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita, Ōita|qq=}}, Ōita) -
